h2h_summary: In their last 5 recorded meetings, Egypt won 2, drew 2, and lost 1 against Australia. Documented matches include a 0-0 result where Egypt lost the tie in the 1987 President's Cup, and a 3-0 victory for Egypt in a 2010 friendly.
wc_history_home: Australia have appeared at six World Cups (1974, 2006–2026), with their best results being two Round of 16 appearances — losing narrowly to eventual champions Italy in 2006 and to eventual champions Argentina in 2022.
wc_history_away: Egypt have appeared at only three previous World Cups (1934, 1990, 2018) and have never won a match or advanced beyond the group stage in any of those tournaments.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: Disciplined back-three defensive structure with Harry Souttar's aerial dominance
Explosive attacking pace through Nestory Irankunda and Martin Boyle on the flanks
High work rate, pressing intensity and set-piece threat throughout the squad
strengths_away: Mohamed Salah and Omar Marmoush provide a world-class attacking partnership capable of deciding games individually
Exceptional defensive record in CAF qualifying — seven clean sheets and only two goals conceded across ten unbeaten matches
Tactical flexibility under Hossam Hassan, shifting between 4-2-3-1 and 3-4-1-2 to control games and frustrate opponents
team_style_home: Australia under Tony Popovic use a back-three system (3-4-2-1 / 3-4-3) with energetic wing-backs, compact defensive organisation, high pressing intensity, and quick direct counter-attacking football rather than dominant possession.
team_style_away: Egypt deploy a compact, disciplined 4-2-3-1 with defensive organization as the priority, using an asymmetric double-pivot in build-up and releasing Mohamed Salah and Omar Marmoush on fast transitions to exploit space behind opposition lines.
recent_news: Egypt have several fitness concerns for Friday’s World Cup last-32 clash with Australia, including Mohamed Salah. Pharaohs captain Salah is among a number of players being assessed after sustaining a hamstring injury during the draw with Iran.
context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
